about.subheading: The lab's anti-fishing discipline: pre-committed specs, structural workflow, and epistemic honesty.

intro.p1: We research before we deploy. Every hedging instrument the lab ships must clear structural-econometrics validation — not post-hoc analysis, not favorable-outcome selection, not narrative.
intro.p2: This page documents the discipline we apply to every iteration. The five stages and four commitments are live: they apply equally to success and failure.

01.title: Spec
01.body: We pre-commit the hypothesis, data universe, instrumental identifiers, and test thresholds BEFORE looking at results. The spec is published as a citable document.

02.title: Data
02.body: We pull the macro and micro panels from the registry fixed in the spec. Any later adjustment — cleaning, imputation, trimming — is documented in a decision-citation block before being applied.

03.title: Estimation
03.body: Structural OLS with HAC standard errors. Robustness checks (window, sub-panel, transform sensitivity) run automatically — they are not optional.

04.title: Tests
04.body: Every code cell passes a trio checkpoint: the cell runs, the outputs are what we expected, and a reviewer confirms. Without the trio, the cell is not considered resolved.

05.title: Disposition
05.body: The iteration closes with a disposition memo: pass, fail, or parked. The memo explains the outcome — and, on rejection, what was learned. Rejection carries equal narrative weight as approval.

commitments.item_2: Trio checkpoint after every code cell
commitments.item_4: Rejection carries equal narrative weight as approval
